current_members.name: Dingyi Pan
current_members.bio: Dr. Dingyi Pan (潘定一) is currently a professor in the Department of Engineering Mechanics, Zhejiang University, Hangzhou, China. He is also affiliated to the State Key Laboratory of Fluid Power & Mechatronic Systems. Dr Pan obtained his Ph.D. degree in fluid mechanics in 2011 from Zhejiang University. He was postdoctoral research fellow in Department of Mechanical Engineering, National University of Singapore from 2011 to 2014. Dr Pan's research interests focus on modeling of complex fluids with mesoscale simulation tools which include coarse-grained molecular dynamics, dissipative particle dynamics, lattice Boltzmann modeling, etc.

current_members.name: Jinhe Wang
current_members.bio:
Jinhe received his Bachelor degree in Engineering Mechanics from Tongji University in 2021. Jinhe is doing some research on the rheology of particle suspensions using fluid-solid coupling computational method. His interest lies in numerical method such as the Lattice Boltzmann method (LBM), immersed boundary method (IBM) and dissipative particle dynamics (DPD) method.
Give Overwatch!

current_members.name: Jiaxin Tong
current_members.bio:
Jiaxin received her Bachelor degree from Shandong University in 2023. During her persuit of Master's degree in Zhejiang University, she will do work on using Artifitial Intelligent method to study the turbulence of non-Newtonian fluid flow. She turned into PhD student in 2025.
Kawayi but cool.

current_members.name: Runyang Qiu
current_members.bio:
Runyang graduated from Xi'an Jiaotong University with a Bachelor degree in Engineering Mechanics (2021), his research interest lies in bubble dynamics and cavitation.
Been to Russia for 1 year, love to Sing, Dance, Rap and Basketball.

current_members.name: Shiyuan Tong
current_members.bio:
Shiyuan received his Bachelor and Master degree from University of Science and Technology of China. During his PhD career, he will work on the turbulence flow of non-Newtonian fluid.
Safety first.
